creator: Sarah Perez
title: (No AI) Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
dc:creator: Sarah Perez
content: Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.
contentSnippet: Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.

creator: Ram Iyer
title: (No AI) Venice AI becomes a unicorn with $65M Series A as its privacy-first AI platform takes off
dc:creator: Ram Iyer
content: Venice AI is already profitable, with annualized run-rate revenues of over $70 million, CEO Erik Voorhees said.
contentSnippet: Venice AI is already profitable, with annualized run-rate revenues of over $70 million, CEO Erik Voorhees said.

title: (No AI) Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/268614_Google_Home_Speaker_JTuohy_0009.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The Google Home Speaker is Google’s first smart speaker in years. And it’s pretty! | Photo: Jennifer Patison Tuohy / The Verge

Smart speakers have spent the past few years searching for a compelling second act. Beyond music, timers, and controlling your lights, they've struggled to justify taking up space on the kitchen counter. [AI promised to change that](https://www.theverge.com/2024/6/14/24177991/apple-intelligence-siri-voice-assistant-amazon-alexa-generative-ai).

Amazon debuted its new [hardware powered by a revamped Alexa](https://www.theverge.com/tech/788051/hands-on-amazon-alexa-echo-show-11-echo-dot-max-echo-studio) last fall, and now it's finally Google's turn. The Google Home Speaker is the company's first new smart speaker in six years and [its first "built for Gemini."](https://www.theverge.com/tech/788102/gemini-for-home-new-google-assistant-launch-date-price-features) After years of neglect, Google appears to be finally getting serious about the smart home - again. The new speaker is the clearest sign yet. [Gemini for Home](https://www.theverge.com/news/762370/google-announces-gemini-for-home-nest-smart-speakers-voice-assistant), however, still feels unfinished.

### Google …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/959503/google-home-speaker-review-gemini-for-home)

title: (No AI) Anthropic’s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
author: Hayden Field
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/STKB364_CLAUDE_2_C_96d15c.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In [a post on X](https://x.com/AnthropicAI/status/2072106151890809341?), Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access Wednesday to users globally on Claude platforms, and that the company would re-enable access on AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry soon, but with no set timeline.

[Anthropic](https://x.com/AnthropicAI/status/2072106151890809341?) wrote:

> We've received notice that the Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
> 
> We'll begin restoring access tomorrow, and will share an update soon.
> 
> We're grateful to our users for their patience, and to everyone who work …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/958964/anthropic-claude-fable-5-is-back)
contentSnippet:
After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access Wednesday to users globally on Claude platforms, and that the company would re-enable access on AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry soon, but with no set timeline.
Anthropic wrote:
We've received notice that the Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
We'll begin restoring access tomorrow, and will share an update soon.
We're grateful to our users for their patience, and to everyone who work …

Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
content: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

creator: Hugo Lowell
title: (No AI) Anthropic Added a New Security Measure to Get Back Into the Trump Administration’s Good Graces
dc:creator: Hugo Lowell
content: The government has removed restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models—but there were strings attached.
contentSnippet: The government has removed restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models—but there were strings attached.

creator: Andy Greenberg
title: (No AI) Claude Helped a Hacker Find a Way to Issue Tickets to Almost Every US Music Festival
dc:creator: Andy Greenberg
content: A researcher found that using Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.7, he could break into the website of Front Gate—used by every festival from Lollapalooza to Bonnaroo—and freely issue any ticket he chose.
contentSnippet: A researcher found that using Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.7, he could break into the website of Front Gate—used by every festival from Lollapalooza to Bonnaroo—and freely issue any ticket he chose.
